mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns do not significantly increase during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces consumer price inflation, real consumer purchasing power expands, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in forward return regressions over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns during mid-cycle expansion show no positive correlation with INDPRO acceleration, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns, with a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth signals broad-based economic strength and rising asset prices, creating a positive feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and GDPC1 yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth during the late stages of an economic cycle reflects peak corporate earnings and investor optimism, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter before a potential downturn.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth in late-cycle regimes fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a negative out-of-sample R-squared over rolling 3-year windows.
